mission: Implement @beep/firecrawl as a schema-first, Effect-first technical driver around the modern Firecrawl v2 SDK, including watcher streams, fake/live Layers, tests, docs, quality closure, PR babysitting, and Greptile 5/5 readiness.

completionGate.statement: Not achieved until this goal's work ships as a PR driven to mergeable via /yeet (bun run beep yeet: repair -> verify -> publish --pr -> monitor).

targetPackage.name: @beep/firecrawl
targetPackage.scaffoldCommand: bun run create-package firecrawl --family drivers --description "Firecrawl driver package"

memory.graphitiSearchDuringPacketAuthoring: timed out for scoped firecrawl driver packet query

methodSurface.excludedRationale: Deprecated v1 compatibility aliases, deprecated scrape-browser aliases, and maintenance-mode extract methods are intentionally outside the required V1 wrapper surface.

keyDecisions.decision: @beep/firecrawl lives in packages/drivers/firecrawl as a flat repo-level driver.
keyDecisions.rationale: Firecrawl is an external SDK/service boundary, not product language or repo-owned foundation substrate.

keyDecisions.decision: Use the modern firecrawl npm package by default.
keyDecisions.rationale: The current npm package is firecrawl; @mendable/firecrawl-js may be used only if the implementation inventory proves firecrawl is not viable.

keyDecisions.decision: Every in-scope method requires Payload, Success, and Failure S.Class schemas.
keyDecisions.rationale: External SDK inputs and outputs are decoded at the technical driver boundary.

keyDecisions.decision: Method Failure classes model decoded API failure shapes; FirecrawlError variants occupy the Effect error channel.
keyDecisions.rationale: Technical failures stay typed and sanitized while consumers translate at product boundaries.

keyDecisions.decision: watcher is a first-class Effect Stream of decoded watcher events.
keyDecisions.rationale: The SDK emits event-driven crawl/batch updates and must preserve finalizer/interruption behavior.

keyDecisions.decision: startExtract, getExtractStatus, and extract are excluded from V1.
keyDecisions.rationale: The local SDK marks extract maintenance-mode deprecated even though live OpenAPI still exposes extract paths.

keyDecisions.decision: PR readiness requires local quality, CI green, actionable comments resolved, and Greptile 5/5.
keyDecisions.rationale: The driver is a broad public technical boundary and must land with high confidence.

phaseStatusMeaning.completed: The phase is documented and its packet-level exit criteria are satisfied.
phaseStatusMeaning.pending: The phase is ready for an implementation agent to execute.
phaseStatusMeaning.blocked: The phase cannot proceed without a named decision, dependency, credential, or external-state change.

phases.name: Export Catalog And Local Quality Closure

qualityCommands: bunx turbo run check test lint type-test --filter=@beep/firecrawl

prClosure.packetCommit: feat: add firecrawl driver goal packet

stopConditions: Firecrawl docs/npm/local source drift makes the method surface ambiguous.
Live integration tests would incur unexpected cost or destructive side effects.
CI failure is unrelated and cannot be reproduced locally after reasonable investigation.
Greptile cannot be triggered or score cannot be observed after documented attempts.
packetVerificationCommands: rg -n "@beep/firecrawl|packages/drivers/firecrawl|watcher|extract|quality-review-fix-loop|greptile|5/5|/home/elpresidank/YeeBois/dev/firecrawl|bun run create-package firecrawl --family drivers" goals/firecrawl-driver
